title: Why did SEELE send Kaworu anyway
display_title: Why did SEELE send Kaworu anyway
wikitext:
[]: Why did SEELE send Kaworu anyway             Quote Post by MaggotMaster » Fri Dec 01, 2006 11:49 am Seriously, Ep 24' makes no sense, besides the fact Kaworu doesn't seem to get the hint when SEELE tell him that Adam is in with Gendo, why the hell did they send him? they apparently were rooting for Shogouki, but if they wanted Kaworu killed, seeing how they say "blah blah EVA UNIT 01 FULFILL YOUR DUTY", why would they even bother sending him after every other angel was dead (or at all, seeing how they could have killed him while he was with SEELE). The other possibility is that they were trying to start 3I since Kaworu = Adam and apparently 3I can be started by "the forbidden union of Adam and Lilith", if we go by Gendo's words in EoE. Any ideas?

[]:             Quote Post by jcmoorehead » Fri Dec 01, 2006 12:00 pm I'm probably wrong but maybe it was a further attempt to break Shinji even more knowing what would happen and that Shinji would have to kill them would prepare Shinji to accept Instrumentality. Like I said I'm probably wrong but thats how I see it anyway.

[]:             Quote Post by MaggotMaster » Fri Dec 01, 2006 12:11 pm Nah, I highly doubt Kaworu was sent with orders to befriend Shinji. That would make their friendship forced and kinda goes against what the show tells us, plus, SEELE don't mention Shinji at all during their chat with Kaworu.

[]:             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Fri Dec 01, 2006 12:31 pm That whole episode makes no sense. Why did he go after Lilith in the first place if he knew Gendo had his real body to begin with? Just WTF was he gonna do with Kyoko? I wonder if Kaworu was always part of their plan, trying to control Adam the same way Gendo was trying to control Lilith. Who knows! EoE makes more sense then 24' does.

[]:             Quote Post by MaggotMaster » Fri Dec 01, 2006 12:39 pm The only reasonable explanation for Kaworu not understanding what SEELE were telling him ("HEY TABRIS! YOUR BODY IS WITH GENDO, YOU KNOW THE DUDE WITH THE MICKEY MOUSE GLOVES. NOT NAILED TO THE GIANT CROSS, YOU IDIOT") would be that Kaworu is not too smart. I mean, he's an angel! he probably doesn't have a Fruit of Knowledge! amirite?

[]:             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Fri Dec 01, 2006 12:51 pm He may have had the Fruit of Knowledge, he is half Lilim after all. I'm sure Anno thought it made sense somehow, sadly we aren't Anno thus don't get it.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Fri Dec 01, 2006 12:53 pm http://evageeks.org/forum/viewtopic.php?p=47732#47732

[]:             Quote Post by MaggotMaster » Fri Dec 01, 2006 1:13 pm Ornette wrote: http://evageeks.org/forum/viewtopic.php?p=47732#47732

[]:             Quote Post by directedition » Fri Dec 01, 2006 4:42 pm Thought it was pretty well understood that was SEELE's intent. The whole point of Instrumentality was to cause and control the third impact. This is why after Kaworu was killed SEELE told the UN that NERV was going to destroy the world and instigated the assault in End of Eva. My question is what happened between 24 and 25. Instrumentality began? How? Kaworu was killed, that should have been the end of it. The only way I could see this as happening is if the last two episodes were intented to go hand-in-hand with End of Eva, which is a whole other ball of wax.

[]:             Quote Post by AuthenticM » Fri Dec 01, 2006 6:15 pm directedition wrote: My question is what happened between 24 and 25. Instrumentality began? How? Kaworu was killed, that should have been the end of it. The only way I could see this as happening is if the last two episodes were intented to go hand-in-hand with End of Eva, which is a whole other ball of wax.

[]:             Quote Post by The Bastard King » Fri Dec 01, 2006 6:48 pm You got a group of crepy old men and a lone, white-haired girly looking boy. They probably did it so no one got any "silly" ideas.

[]:             Quote Post by directedition » Fri Dec 01, 2006 7:04 pm AuthenticM wrote: The last two episodes do go hand-in-hand with EoE. 25&26 and EoE are two sides of the same coin.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Fri Dec 01, 2006 7:16 pm directedition wrote: Is this established fact now? Considering the vastly different endings, I think it's still open ended.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Fri Dec 01, 2006 7:20 pm directedition wrote: Thought it was pretty well understood that was SEELE's intent. The whole point of Instrumentality was to cause and control the third impact. This is why after Kaworu was killed SEELE told the UN that NERV was going to destroy the world and instigated the assault in End of Eva.

[]:             Quote Post by directedition » Fri Dec 01, 2006 7:28 pm Ornette wrote: If it was their plan to have Kaworu set off 3rd impact, then what about killing the 15 angels? Why bother killing any of them, just let them start 3rd impact if that's really what they were doing.

[]:             Quote Post by thewayneiac » Fri Dec 01, 2006 8:05 pm MaggotMaster wrote: The only reasonable explanation for Kaworu not understanding what SEELE were telling him ("HEY TABRIS! YOUR BODY IS WITH GENDO, YOU KNOW THE DUDE WITH THE MICKEY MOUSE GLOVES. NOT NAILED TO THE GIANT CROSS, YOU IDIOT") would be that Kaworu is not too smart. I mean, he's an angel! he probably doesn't have a Fruit of Knowledge! amirite?

[]:             Quote Post by directedition » Fri Dec 01, 2006 8:16 pm thewayneiac wrote: I still think that Shin-seiki has the best explanation for this: Kaworu did understand that Adam's body was with Gendo, and knew all the time that it was Lilith on the cross. He also knew that Rei was watching and was play-acting confusion for her benefit. He wanted Rei to know it was Lilith because he was trying to give her a clue as to her identity, just as he was earlier when he told her they had both taken the Lilims' form. I've dubbed this the "Casablanca" theory, with Kaworu in the Claude Raines role: "I'm Shocked Shinji, shocked to find Lilith here!".

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Thu Dec 07, 2006 5:42 pm jcmoorehead wrote: I'm probably wrong but maybe it was a further attempt to break Shinji even more knowing what would happen and that Shinji would have to kill them would prepare Shinji to accept Instrumentality.

[]:             Quote Post by jakewashere » Thu Dec 07, 2006 6:06 pm ObsessiveMathsFreak wrote: jcmoorehead wrote: I'm probably wrong but maybe it was a further attempt to break Shinji even more knowing what would happen and that Shinji would have to kill them would prepare Shinji to accept Instrumentality.

[]:             Quote Post by MaggotMaster » Thu Dec 07, 2006 6:24 pm As cool as ObsessiveMathsFreak's theory sounds I think they were expecting more from Kaworu than just his death, plus SEELE never mention Shinji to Kaworu and in fact the only time they ever mention him is in EoE and at the start of Ep 20, that's twice in the entire series , if he was that important I think there would be more evidence to support it, plus you know, the series tells us that everyone has an imperfect ego, so I think Shinji just happened to be there and wasn't truly a fundamental part of SEELE's plans for instrumentality, Shogoki was. Also, consider the fact that the JSSDF (under SEELE's orders) tries to kill Shinji, if SEELE had planned ahead of time to use him for 3I they wouldn't of had the JSSDF try to kill every Eva pilot .

 in fact the only time they ever mention him is in EoE and at the start of Ep 20, that's twice in the entire series , if he was that important I think there would be more evidence to support it, plus you know, the series tells us that everyone has an imperfect ego, so I think Shinji just happened to be there and wasn't truly a fundamental part of SEELE's plans for instrumentality, Shogoki was.
